analysis.summary: WooCommerce is a comprehensive e-commerce platform built as a WordPress plugin, providing a complete online store solution with product management, cart and checkout functionality, payment processing, and extensive customization capabilities. The system follows a monorepo architecture with modular PHP and TypeScript packages, implementing a plugin-based extensibility model that allows third-party developers to extend core functionality through hooks, filters, and custom blocks.
capabilities: Manages product catalogs with variants, attributes, categories, and inventory tracking (plugins/woocommerce/includes/class-wc-product.php)
Processes shopping cart operations with session management and item calculations (plugins/woocommerce/includes/class-wc-cart.php)
Handles checkout flow with customer data validation and order processing (plugins/woocommerce/includes/class-wc-checkout.php)
Integrates multiple payment gateways through extensible payment method API (plugins/woocommerce/includes/abstracts/abstract-wc-payment-gateway.php)
Provides REST API endpoints for external integrations and mobile apps (plugins/woocommerce/includes/rest-api/)
Implements Gutenberg blocks for product display and checkout functionality (packages/js/components/src/)
Manages order lifecycle from creation to fulfillment with status tracking (plugins/woocommerce/includes/class-wc-order.php)
Handles customer account management with registration and authentication (plugins/woocommerce/includes/class-wc-customer.php)
Processes shipping calculations with multiple carrier integrations (plugins/woocommerce/includes/class-wc-shipping.php)
Manages tax calculations with configurable rates and rules (plugins/woocommerce/includes/class-wc-tax.php)
Provides comprehensive reporting and analytics dashboard (packages/js/components/src/analytics/)
Implements coupon and discount system with flexible rule engine (plugins/woocommerce/includes/class-wc-coupon.php)
Manages inventory tracking with stock status and backorder handling (plugins/woocommerce/includes/class-wc-product-variation.php)
Provides multi-currency support with exchange rate management (plugins/woocommerce/includes/class-wc-currencies.php)
Implements webhook system for real-time event notifications (plugins/woocommerce/includes/class-wc-webhook.php)
Manages email notifications for orders, customers, and admin events (plugins/woocommerce/includes/emails/)
Provides admin interface for store configuration and management (plugins/woocommerce/includes/admin/)
Implements data stores abstraction for flexible data persistence (plugins/woocommerce/includes/data-stores/)
Handles file uploads and digital product delivery (plugins/woocommerce/includes/class-wc-download-handler.php)
Provides extensive hook system for third-party customization (plugins/woocommerce/includes/wc-core-functions.php)
Implements block-based checkout with React components (packages/js/components/src/)
Manages subscription and recurring payment processing (plugins/woocommerce/includes/class-wc-subscriptions.php)
Provides multilingual and localization support (plugins/woocommerce/i18n/)
Implements caching strategies for performance optimization (plugins/woocommerce/includes/class-wc-cache-helper.php)
Manages GDPR compliance with data export and deletion tools (plugins/woocommerce/includes/class-wc-privacy.php)
use_cases: E-commerce store owners use this to create and manage online shops with complete product catalogs, payment processing, and order management
Developers use this to build custom e-commerce solutions and extend functionality through plugins and themes
Digital agencies use this to deliver turnkey e-commerce websites for their clients with customized branding and features
SaaS companies integrate with the REST API to build e-commerce applications and mobile commerce solutions
Enterprise businesses use this as a foundation for complex multi-store and B2B commerce platforms
analysis.target_audience: WordPress developers, E-commerce store owners, Digital agencies, SaaS developers, Extension developers
api_endpoints: Based on Store API documentation structure - exact endpoints would need to be extracted from route definition files
Cart management endpoints for adding, updating, removing items
Checkout endpoints for processing orders and payments
Product endpoints for catalog browsing and search
Order endpoints for order tracking and management
auth_methods: REST API authentication with API keys
Session-based authentication for cart and checkout
integrations: MySQL/MariaDB database via WordPress WPDB (docker-compose.yml shows MariaDB 10.5)
Payment gateway integrations through abstract payment gateway class
Shipping provider integrations through shipping method API

architecture.pattern: monorepo with multiple services - contains plugins, packages, tools, and documentation
architecture.data_flow: Requests flow through WordPress hooks system to WooCommerce core, which processes business logic through data stores, communicates with database via WordPress WPDB abstraction, and returns responses through REST API or renders WordPress admin pages. Frontend uses React components that interact with Store API endpoints.
architecture.persistence: WordPress database tables with custom WooCommerce tables, uses WordPress WPDB abstraction layer, data stores pattern for flexible persistence
architecture.concurrency_model: PHP synchronous processing with WordPress cron for background tasks, React event-loop for frontend components
architecture.communication_patterns: HTTP REST API, WordPress hooks and filters system, React component props and state management
architecture.plugin_extension_model: WordPress plugin architecture with extensive hook system, allows third-party plugins to extend functionality through actions and filters, supports custom post types and taxonomies

primary_entities: Orders with line items and metadata
Customers with billing and shipping addresses
Coupons with usage rules and restrictions
storage_backends: File system for uploads and logs
data_model.schema_patterns: Relational database with WordPress custom post types, meta tables for flexible attributes, custom tables for WooCommerce-specific data
data_model.migration_strategy: WordPress database upgrade system with version tracking and automatic migrations

algorithms.problem_type: E-commerce transaction processing with inventory management, tax calculation, shipping cost computation, and payment processing workflows
core_patterns: Data Store pattern for persistence abstraction
Template Method pattern for email notifications
algorithms.complexity_indicators: Large codebase with 3729 PHP files and 2000+ TypeScript files, complex business logic for e-commerce operations, extensive customization capabilities
key_algorithms: Tax calculation with multiple rate types and compound taxes
Shipping cost calculation with zone-based pricing
Price calculation with discounts and coupons
Order processing workflow with status transitions

security_model.authentication: WordPress native authentication system with session management
security_model.authorization: WordPress capabilities and roles system, custom WooCommerce capabilities for store management
security_model.data_protection: WordPress nonce system for CSRF protection, data sanitization and validation throughout
security_model.security_headers: Relies on WordPress core security headers and practices
security_model.vulnerability_handling: HackerOne security reporting program (SECURITY.md), regular security updates through WordPress plugin update system

deployment.ci_cd: GitHub Actions workflows (implied by monorepo structure and PNPM usage)
deployment.environment_config: WordPress configuration system, environment variables for OAuth and API keys (.env.sample files)
deployment.scaling_model: Horizontal scaling through WordPress multisite, vertical scaling through server resources

code_quality_signals.test_framework: Jest for JavaScript/TypeScript components, PHPUnit implied for PHP code
code_quality_signals.code_style: WordPress coding standards, ESLint, Prettier, SASS linting

project_health.recent_activity: Performance optimization for cart data in Store API, indicating active development on core e-commerce functionality
project_health.community_signals: Code of conduct, security policy, comprehensive documentation, contributor guidelines, release process documentation

information_gaps: Exact REST API endpoint definitions would require examining route definition files
Complete dependency list would need full composer.json and package.json analysis
Database schema details would need examination of migration files
Performance characteristics would need benchmarking data

functional_dna.logic_archetype_evidence: The system implements an extensive plugin architecture with hooks, filters, and extensible components. The core provides a foundation framework while allowing third-party developers to extend functionality through standardized APIs and event systems.
functional_dna.data_contract_evidence: Evidence: REST API endpoints with defined schemas, WordPress database schema with custom tables, but extensive use of meta tables and flexible attributes for customization. PHP type hints and TypeScript definitions provide some structure.
functional_dna.core_algorithmic_evidence: Evidence from extensive database operations (products, orders, customers), session management, inventory tracking, and state transitions. Complex business rule processing for tax calculations, shipping costs, and discount applications.
functional_dna.concurrency_evidence: Evidence from WordPress database architecture, session-based cart management, and background cron processing. No strict ACID requirements across all operations, allows for eventual consistency in inventory and reporting.
functional_dna.abstract_problem: Manages complex hierarchical entity relationships with customizable attributes, extensible business rule processing, and third-party integration points while maintaining transactional integrity for state-changing operations.
functional_dna.data_flow_pattern: Request-Response with side-effect persistence and event-driven hooks → business logic processing → database mutations → notification dispatch
functional_dna.repurposing_reasoning: The core patterns of extensible business logic, entity management with flexible attributes, and plugin architecture are highly reusable. The hook/filter system, data store abstraction, and modular component structure could apply to many domains. However, specific business logic like tax calculations and shipping are domain-locked. The WordPress dependency and PHP architecture create some migration friction but the underlying patterns are broadly applicable.

evidence_claims.evidence: plugins/woocommerce/includes/wc-core-functions.php contains extensive hook system, abstract classes for payment gateways and shipping methods

evidence_claims.claim: Uses data store abstraction pattern for flexible persistence

evidence_claims.evidence: Tax calculation (class-wc-tax.php), shipping calculations (class-wc-shipping.php), coupon rules (class-wc-coupon.php)

evidence_claims.claim: Implements REST API with structured endpoints
evidence_claims.evidence: plugins/woocommerce/includes/rest-api/ directory and Store API documentation structure

cross_industry_applications: Healthcare Practice Management - patient records, appointment scheduling, billing rules
Real Estate Property Management - property listings, tenant management, lease processing
Educational Course Management - course catalogs, enrollment, grading systems
Event Management Platforms - event listings, ticket sales, attendee management
Professional Services Billing - client management, time tracking, invoice generation
Manufacturing Order Management - product configurations, order processing, inventory
Content Management Systems - publishable content, user permissions, workflow states
Subscription/SaaS Billing - recurring billing, plan management, usage tracking

complexity_factors: WordPress core dependency creates platform lock-in
Extensive hook system requires understanding WordPress patterns
Complex database schema with custom tables and meta relationships
Large codebase with 3729 PHP files requiring selective extraction
friction_report.migration_path: Extract core data store patterns and business logic processors, reimplement hook system in target framework, migrate database schema design patterns, rebuild REST API layer

output.delivery: sync for API, async for webhooks and email notifications

authentication.method: WordPress native authentication with REST API token support

comparable_systems: Drupal Commerce - CMS-based extensible business platform with plugin architecture
SugarCRM - Customer relationship management with customizable entities and workflows
Moodle Learning Management System - Educational platform with extensive plugin ecosystem
JIRA/Atlassian Platform - Issue tracking with customizable workflows and third-party integrations
Salesforce Platform - Business application platform with custom objects and apex extensions
information_gaps: Exact REST API endpoint definitions and request/response schemas
Complete database schema with all custom tables and relationships
Specific webhook event types and payload structures
Internal caching strategies and optimization techniques
